creator: andreas@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Sun, Jan 07, 2001 at 08:44:18PM +0200, Peter Pentchev wrote:
> On Sat, Jan 06, 2001 at 05:06:33PM +0100, andreas@FreeBSD.org wrote:
> [snip]
> > --- Makefile.orig	Sat Jan  6 16:48:50 2001
> > +++ Makefile	Sat Jan  6 16:50:22 2001
> > @@ -38,7 +38,7 @@
> >  #	-O3   - optimize (it seems that -O3 also help to find out some errors);
> >  #	-g    - produce debugging information.
> >  #
> > -CFLAGS=-Wall -O3 -g
> > +#CFLAGS=-Wall -O3 -g
> 
> Wouldn't this be better off with something like:
> 
> CFLAGS ?= -Wall -O3 -g
> 
> That's the way it's done in other ports; that way, it allows the user
> to manually build/test software from within ${WRKSRC} without having
> to make CFLAGS='...' each time.

Good idea.

	Andreas ///

-- 
Andreas Klemm - Powered by FreeBSD 4.2 SMP
